etymology.description: 陳(陳) (chén, "display; aged") refers to being laid out or aged. 皮(皮) (pí, "skin; peel") refers to the outer layer or skin of fruits. Together, 陳皮(陳皮) (chénpí, "dried tangerine peel") implies the concept of the aged or dried skin of tangerines.
etymology.interpretation: 陳(陳) (chén, "display; aged") suggests something that has been laid out, often indicating the passage of time and thereby aging, which aligns with the drying process. 皮(皮) (pí, "skin; peel") literally refers to the peel of a fruit. The combination denotes the peel of tangerines that has been allowed to dry out with the passage of time, a common practice in Chinese culinary and medicinal traditions.

examples.chinese: 陈皮(陳皮)是传统中药的一种。
examples.pinyin: Chénpí shì chuántǒng zhōngyào de yī zhǒng.
examples.english: Dried tangerine peel is a type of traditional Chinese medicine.

examples.chinese: 我们常用陈皮(我們常用陳皮)来煮汤。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen chángyòng chénpí lái zhǔ tāng.
examples.english: We often use dried tangerine peel to make soup.

examples.chinese: 在冬天(在冬天)，陈皮茶(陳皮茶)能驱寒(能驅寒)。
examples.pinyin: Zài dōngtiān, chénpí chá néng qū hán.
examples.english: In winter, dried tangerine peel tea can ward off the cold.
